perturbation.original_text: 1.16. PaperExchange Competitor shall mean any exchange, auction or reverse auction for the sale, purchase and/or exchange of pulp, paper and paper packaging.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.16. PaperExchange Competitor shall mean any exchange, auction or reverse auction for the sale, purchase and/or exchange of pulp, paper and paper packaging, except those operated by VerticalNet or its affiliates.
perturbation.explanation: This change modifies the definition of "PaperExchange Competitor" to exclude exchanges operated by VerticalNet. This contradicts Section 4.3.1, which restricts VerticalNet from acting as an advertising agent or representative for *any* PaperExchange Competitor, creating uncertainty regarding whether VerticalNet can act as an agent for its own exchanges.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.3.1. During the Term, VerticalNet shall not (a) act as an advertising agent or representative for any PaperExchange Competitor and (b) place any advertisements on Pulp and Paper Online from any PaperExchange Competitor.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly excludes VerticalNet's own exchanges from the definition of 'PaperExchange Competitor'. This creates a direct contradiction with Section 4.3.1, which prohibits VerticalNet from acting as an advertising agent for *any* PaperExchange Competitor. The modified definition allows VerticalNet to potentially act as an agent for its own exchanges, directly conflicting with the broad prohibition in Section 4.3.1. This creates a clear in-text contradiction that could lead to disputes about the scope of the restriction imposed on VerticalNet.

perturbation.original_text: 1.21. PaperExchange Mark shall mean any trademark, service mark, trade name, domain name, design or logo of PaperExchange.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.21. PaperExchange Mark shall mean any trademark, service mark, trade name, domain name, design or logo of PaperExchange, except where jointly registered under Co-Branded Sites.
perturbation.explanation: The revised definition of "PaperExchange Mark" now excludes marks that are jointly registered under the Co-Branded Sites. This creates a contradiction with Section 6.4, which grants VerticalNet a license to use *any* PaperExchange Link. It becomes unclear whether VerticalNet can use marks jointly registered under the Co-Branded Sites, which might be argued to fall outside the definition of "PaperExchange Mark" due to the exception introduced here.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6.4. PaperExchange hereby grants VerticalNet a non-exclusive, non-transferable, royalty-free, right and license to link to the PaperExchange Site through a PaperExchange Link. PaperExchange shall furnish VerticalNet with a full color representation of each PaperExchange Link at least two days prior to its scheduled placement on Pulp and Paper Online. If PaperExchange subsequently modifies any PaperExchange Link or the URL associated with such PaperExchange Link, it shall furnish a representation of same to VerticalNet, which VerticalNet shall substitute for the prior version within two days after receipt thereof. PaperExchange shall have final approval over all PaperExchange Links on Pulp and Paper Online.
perturbation.justification: The modification to Section 1.21, which now excludes jointly registered marks under Co-Branded Sites from the definition of "PaperExchange Mark", creates a direct contradiction with Section 6.4. Section 6.4 grants VerticalNet a license to use *any* PaperExchange Link. Because the modified definition now potentially excludes some PaperExchange Marks (those jointly registered), it is unclear whether VerticalNet's license extends to these excluded marks, thus creating a direct conflict in the contract and a structural flaw causing an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 1.36. VerticalNet Mark shall mean any trademark, service mark, trade name, domain name, design or logo of VerticalNet.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.36. VerticalNet Mark shall mean any trademark, service mark, trade name, domain name, design or logo of VerticalNet, unless otherwise agreed in writing.
perturbation.explanation: By adding "unless otherwise agreed in writing" to the definition of "VerticalNet Mark", we create a direct conflict with Section 6.5 which states that the Intellectual Property of VerticalNet is and shall remain the sole property of VerticalNet. This change implies that there may be situations where the VerticalNet Marks are not solely VerticalNet's, contradicting the original intent. This adds ambiguity to the rights associated with the marks and could affect how PaperExchange uses them under the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6.5. Except for the express rights granted to PaperExchange under this Agreement, PaperExchange acknowledges and agrees that the Intellectual Property of VerticalNet is and shall remain the sole property of VerticalNet and nothing in this Agreement shall confer in PaperExchange any right of ownership or license rights in VerticalNet's Intellectual Property. In addition, PaperExchange shall not now or in the future contest the validity of VerticalNet's Intellectual Property.
perturbation.justification: The addition of "unless otherwise agreed in writing" to the definition of "VerticalNet Mark" directly contradicts section 6.5, which states that VerticalNet's Intellectual Property "is and shall remain the sole property of VerticalNet". The changed text implies that VerticalNet Marks may not always be the sole property of VerticalNet, creating a direct conflict and ambiguity regarding ownership, which directly breaks compatibility and creates an enforceable issue in the document.
